prompts.goal: i want to fix my sleep schedule

quiz_answers.What do you find to be your biggest chal: Staying up late due to distractions
quiz_answers.Additional context: i go to bed at 12am and it takes me not too long to fall asleep after that, but i want to sleep earlier

Sleep Environment: Limit exposure to screens before bed
Lifestyle Adjustments: Reduce caffeine intake in the afternoon

Establish a consistent bedtime.task: Track your current bedtimes for a week to understand your sleep patterns.
Set a bedtime reminder on your phone for 15 minutes before your desired sleep time.
Choose one distraction (like phone or TV) to turn off 30 minutes before your targeted bedtime for a whole week.
Reduce your bedtime by 15 minutes for one week and maintain that new time.
Establish a relaxing bedtime routine (e.g., reading or meditation) for 10-15 minutes before sleep for two weeks.
Achieve a consistent bedtime of no later than 11 PM for three consecutive weeks while maintaining your new routine.

Create a relaxing pre-sleep routine.task: Identify one distraction that keeps you up late and take note of it. Aim to reduce this distraction by at least 10 minutes each night.
Establish a consistent bedtime for the next week (set an alarm to remind you 15 minutes before). Aim to go to bed at the same time every night.
Create a wind-down activity (like reading or meditating) that lasts at least 10 minutes before bed. Practice this activity every night for a week.
Limit screen time (TV, phone, computer) to 30 minutes before your new bedtime for 5 consecutive nights. Keep a journal of how you feel each morning.
Design a personalized pre-sleep routine that includes at least three relaxing activities (such as a warm bath, light stretching, or calming music). Follow this routine nightly for one week.
Master your new sleep routine by successfully going to bed at your target time for 14 consecutive nights. Reflect on the changes you notice in your sleep quality and daytime energy levels.

Optimize bedroom comfort.task: Declutter your bedroom by removing at least 5 items that are not used for sleeping or relaxation.
Create a calming bedtime routine by spending 15 minutes each evening winding down, involving activities like reading or meditating.
Adjust your bedroom temperature to a comfortable level (ideally between 60-67°F or 15-19°C) before going to bed for a week.
Experiment with blackout curtains or eye masks to eliminate light in your bedroom for at least 4 nights this week.
Limit screen time in your bedroom by designating a 'no electronics' zone or time for 5 consecutive evenings.
Create a personalized sleep sanctuary by rearranging your bedroom layout, optimizing for comfort, and incorporating calming colors or decor. Maintain this setup for 30 days.

Limit exposure to screens before bed.task: Set a reminder on your phone to start winding down for bed at 11:30 PM. Use a gentle tone or a calming notification sound.
Limit exposure to screens before bed.id: Limit exposure to screens before bed_0

Limit exposure to screens before bed.task: Turn off all screens (phone, computer, TV) 30 minutes before your bedtime. Use this time to read a book or practice a relaxing activity.
Limit exposure to screens before bed.id: Limit exposure to screens before bed_1

Limit exposure to screens before bed.task: Replace one screen-based activity with a non-screen activity for an additional 30 minutes before bedtime, such as meditating or journaling.
Limit exposure to screens before bed.id: Limit exposure to screens before bed_2

Limit exposure to screens before bed.task: Create a relaxing bedtime routine that you follow every night for at least a week. Include at least three non-screen activities like stretching, reading, or listening to calming music.
Limit exposure to screens before bed.id: Limit exposure to screens before bed_3

Limit exposure to screens before bed.task: Reduce your overall screen time during the day by 1 hour. Use this time for activities that promote relaxation and wind down more effectively at night.
Limit exposure to screens before bed.id: Limit exposure to screens before bed_4

Limit exposure to screens before bed.task: Maintain a consistent sleep schedule by going to bed no later than 11 PM and waking up the same time every day for 2 weeks, ensuring you limit screen exposure in the last hour before sleep.
Limit exposure to screens before bed.id: Limit exposure to screens before bed_5

Reduce caffeine intake in the afternoon.task: Track your caffeine intake for one week. Write down every caffeinated drink you consume in the afternoon.
Reduce caffeine intake in the afternoon.id: Reduce caffeine intake in the afternoon_0

Reduce caffeine intake in the afternoon.task: Identify and eliminate one source of caffeine in the afternoon this week (e.g., switching from coffee to herbal tea).
Reduce caffeine intake in the afternoon.id: Reduce caffeine intake in the afternoon_1

Reduce caffeine intake in the afternoon.task: Replace caffeinated drinks with non-caffeinated options for two consecutive afternoons this week.
Reduce caffeine intake in the afternoon.id: Reduce caffeine intake in the afternoon_2

Reduce caffeine intake in the afternoon.task: Reduce your total caffeine intake by half during the afternoon for an entire week.
Reduce caffeine intake in the afternoon.id: Reduce caffeine intake in the afternoon_3

Reduce caffeine intake in the afternoon.task: Go caffeine-free in the afternoon for one full week, focusing on alternatives like water or decaffeinated drinks.
Reduce caffeine intake in the afternoon.id: Reduce caffeine intake in the afternoon_4

Reduce caffeine intake in the afternoon.task: Maintain a caffeine-free afternoon schedule for a month, and write a reflection on how it has impacted your sleep and energy levels.
Reduce caffeine intake in the afternoon.id: Reduce caffeine intake in the afternoon_5

Incorporate regular physical activity.task: Take a 10-minute walk after dinner to unwind and incorporate light physical activity into your evening routine.
Add a 15-minute stretching or yoga session to your morning routine, aiming to do this at least 3 times this week.
Engage in a 20-minute workout (could be bodyweight exercises, a home fitness video, or a quick run) at least 2 times this week.
Increase your activity by incorporating 30 minutes of moderate exercise (like brisk walking, cycling, or dancing) at least 3 times this week.
Join a local fitness class or group activity (like yoga, martial arts, or team sports) and commit to attending at least once a week for a month.
Create a balanced weekly exercise plan that includes a mix of cardio, strength training, and flexibility exercises, and follow it consistently for one month.

bonus_quests.task: Create a 'Wind Down' Routine: Dedicate 15 minutes before bed to a calming activity like reading, meditating, or journaling. Stick to this routine for a week to signal to your body that it's time to sleep earlier.

Digital Curfew: Set a digital curfew for yourself where all screens (phone, computer, TV) turn off by 11 PM. Use the last hour before bed for relaxing activities instead. Track your adherence for a week.
Sleep Challenge: Try setting your bedtime earlier by 15 minutes each night until you reach your ideal sleep time. Document how you feel each morning during this transition for a week.
